rulings.Case Title: EMALATA TAKAPU VS. OPTIONE HEALTHCARE MANAGEMENT LLC A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF EMALATA TAKAPU's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Plaintiff Emalata Takapu's motion to strike Defendant OptionONE Healthcare Management LLC's answer is granted. No opposition filed, and good cause shown. OptionONE is not represented by counsel. The Court previously granted Plaintiff's motion to strike OptionONE's answer to a prior complaint, but granted OptionONE the opportunity to cure the defect by retaining counsel to represent it. OptionONE having failed to do so within a reasonable period of time, its answer to the second amended complaint filed Feb. 13, 2019 is stricken. (See CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1149.) rulings.Case Title: EILEEN STAFSBERG ET AL VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 15, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS EILEEN STAFSBERG, TRACY GRIFFIn'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELLIOT TAYLOR ET AL VS. MICHAEL CALLAHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT COLTON CALLAHAN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Colton Callahan's demurrer to Plaintiffs Elliot Taylor, Ron Taylor and 2441-2449 2nd Street LLC's second amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. There are no facts pled in the second amended complaint that are inconsistent with the unverified original complaint. Plaintiffs selectively quote from the original complaint, which alleged that "since Defendants refused to provide any of their required financing the Plainiffs decided to fund the project entirely by themselves." (Compl. 11 [emphasis added].) The added allegations in the second amended complaint regarding an oral contract are not inconsistent with the allegations in the original complaint. (See also id. 6, 7, 8, 9(d).) "Critically, the sham pleading doctrine 'cannot be mechanically applied.'" (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (Mar. 28, 2019) 2019 WL 1396740 at *8. In particular, the doctrine "was not intended to preclude plaintiffs from providing additional and noncontradictory allegations." (Leasequip, Inc. v. Dapeer (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 394, 405 n.6; see also Zakk v. Diesel (Mar. 25, 2019) 2019 WL 1349530 at *11-12 [allegations regarding contract claims in third amended complaint "merely clarifies the basis for [plaintiff's] claims for relief" and sham pleading doctrine therefore did not apply].) Likewise, the fraud allegations in the second amended complaint are not inconsistent with the allegation in the original complaint that both Michel Colton Callahan and Colton Callahan misrepresented their ability to fund the project and that they were each other's agents. (Compl. 5,6.) Nor has Plaintiff failed to plead the alleged misrepresentations about the Callahans' ability to finance the project with particularity. (SAC 8.) Finally, Plaintiff has pled that he is a co-manager of ST6 Holdings, LLC and therefore has standing pursuant to Corp. Code § 17707.03(a) to bring an action for its dissolution. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ELLIOT TAYLOR ET AL VS. MICHAEL CALLAHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT COLTON CALLAHAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Colton Callahan's motion to strike portions of plaintiffs Elliot Taylor, Ron Taylor and 2441-2449 2nd Street LLC's second amended complaint is granted in part and denied in part. The prayer for attorney's fees is stricken pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 435(b) because it is not supported by any allegation as to a contract or statute authorizing such fees. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1033.5.) Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend if he can allege in good faith a contractual or statutory basis supporting recovery of attorney's fees. The prayer for punitive damages is supported by the fraud allegations and the motion to strike this portion of the complaint is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAMEL BOUZIDI ET AL VS. RAOUF BOUZIDI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Complaint (Tentative Ruling 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2019, Line 9, Demurrer To Complaint (Tentative Ruling 2 Of 2) Although there is a split of authority as to whether unjust enrichment is a cause of action, it is routinely pled as a restitution or quasi contract theory of liability. (Durell v. Sharp Healthcare (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1350, 1370.) The elements of a cause of action for unjust enrichment are simply stated as receipt of a benefit and unjust retention of the benefit at the expense of another. (Professional Tax Appeal (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 230, 238.) The complaint adequately pleads those elements. (Compl. 22-23.) However, in order to assert a claim for restitution based on unjust enrichment, Plaintiffs must make it clear that the claim is an inconsistent claim alleging the absence of an enforceable agreement. (See Klein v. Chevron USA, Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAMEL BOUZIDI ET AL VS. RAOUF BOUZIDI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT RAOUF BOUZIDI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (Tentative Ruling 1 of 2) Defendant Raouf Bouzidi's demurrer to plaintiffs Jamel Bouzidi and Sofiene Bouzidi's complaint is overruled as to the breach of contract cause of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the unjust enrichment cause of action. Plaintiffs have alleged the existence of a valid contract. On its face, the settlement agreement does not have the unlawful object of circumventing federal income tax law; rather, it provides that defendant is "solely liable and responsible for any and all taxes, penalties, fines, interest, costs, fees, and attorney's fees" in relation to the operation of the restaurant business Chez Fayala, LLC, including any tax liability that Plaintiffs shall have for that business beyond their salary or W-2 earnings, and further that he shall indemnify Plantiffs against any claims arising out of the operation of that business. (Settlement Agreement and Release 3.2.1, 3.2.2.). Defendant cites no authority finding unlawful an agreement to assume responsibility for tax liabilities. " 'Whether a contract is illegal . . . is a question of law to be determined from the circumstances of each particular case.'" (Kashani v. Tsann Kuen China Enterprise Co. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 531, 540.) Further, " '[a]s a general rule, if a contract can be performed legally, a court will presume that the parties intended a lawful mode of performance.'" (Id. at 549.) The contract is not unlawful on its face under Civ. Code §§ 1667, 2773 and 2774 under the particular facts alleged in this case, where the settlement agreement incorporates a sworn declaration by Defendant admitting that he was solely responsible for all decisions and actions related to all of the operations of Chez Fayala LLC, Plaintiffs did not benefit from his actions, and Defendant has sole responsibility and liability for any taxes, penalties and fines regarding the operation of Chez Fayala, LLC from its inception until its winding down. While the provision of the settlement agreement referring to indemnification against criminal liability for tax fraud or evasion or criminal money laundering is likely unenforceable, the complaint does not allege that the breach of contract arises out of any such facts; it alleges only that upon the conclusion of a federal tax audit, "an amount for federal income taxes owed was determined" which Defendant has refused to pay. (Compl. 15.) Nor has Defendant established that provision cannot be severed. "Where the consideration is only partly illegal and the agreement is severable, the legal portion may be enforced." (1 Witkin, Summary of California Law, Contracts (11th ed. 2018) § 422; see also id. § 423 [liberal rule of severability].)=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRADY LAW GROUP, INC. VS. HASSELL LAW GROUP INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT DAWN HASSELL, HASSELL LAW GROUP, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION's For Stay. Defendants Hassell Law Group, Inc. and Dawn L. Hassell's motion for stay is granted in part. The action is temporarily stayed pending a determination by the Bar Association of San Francisco whether to dismiss the arbitration Defendants initiated against Mr. Corbelli. If BASF does not dismiss the arbitration and those proceedings go forward, there is a risk of inconsistent rulings and duplicative and burdensome proceedings; accordingly, a brief stay is appropriate. If BASF dismisses the arbitration, the stay shall terminate by its terms without the need for a further order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRADY LAW GROUP, INC. VS. HASSELL LAW GROUP INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 15 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF BRADY LAW GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Sanctions. Plaintiff Brady Law Group, Inc.'s motion for sanctions is denied without prejudice to its renewal after both parties have had a reasonable opportunity to file pleading motions, to conduct discovery, and/or to seek summary judgment or summary adjudication as to the claims in the cross-complaint. The request in the reply brief that the Court dismiss the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and strike the prayer for punitive damages is denied as beyond the scope of the relief sought in the notice of motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
